 {"id":"franken_numpy-tqa1","title":"numpy.polyint python wrapper parity","description":"Add fnp_python.polyint passthrough wrapper around numpy.polyint(p, m=1, k=None). Returns the m-th antiderivative of polynomial p with integration constants k. Include oracle test covering: integral of constant c → c·x, x → x²/2, x²+1 → x³/3 + x, m=2 with k=[c1,c2], default k=0, polyder(polyint(p)) round-trip, and complex coefficients.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:52:10.331474802Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T09:52:10.331474802Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_numpy-tteu","title":"numpy.spacing python wrapper parity","description":"Add fnp_python.spacing passthrough wrapper around numpy.spacing(x). Returns the distance to the next floating-point representable number. Include oracle test covering: spacing(1.0) ≈ ulp(1.0) = 2.22e-16, spacing(0) = smallest subnormal, scalar negative input, 1-D array, integer input promoted to float, and verifying spacing(x) > 0 for finite x.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:39:39.009523616Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T09:39:39.009523616Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_numpy-tutl","title":"numpy.flipud python wrapper parity","description":"Add fnp_python.flipud passthrough wrapper around numpy.flipud(m). Reverses elements in up/down direction (axis=0). Include oracle test covering: 1-D reversal (matches axis=0 flip), 2-D row reversal, 3-D leaving non-leading axes intact, dtype preservation, and ValueError on 0-D input matching numpy.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:15:30.169572092Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T08:20:38.962854984Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T08:20:38.961024159Z","close_reason":"Completed in 8c52907: numpy.flipud wrapper with 6-scenario oracle test covering 1-D/2-D/3-D, dtype, 0-D error parity, and flip(axis=0) cross-check","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"franken_numpy-txex","title":"numpy.gradient python wrapper parity","description":"Add fnp_python.gradient passthrough wrapper around numpy.gradient(f, *varargs, axis=None, edge_order=1). Include oracle test covering: 1-D unit-spacing returning central differences, explicit dx scalar spacing, 2-D returning a list of two arrays, axis selection, edge_order=2 second-order accurate boundaries, and integer input promoted to float.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:06:15.316720857Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T07:06:15.316720857Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"franken_numpy-txex","title":"numpy.gradient python wrapper parity","description":"Add fnp_python.gradient passthrough wrapper around numpy.gradient(f, *varargs, axis=None, edge_order=1). Include oracle test covering: 1-D unit-spacing returning central differences, explicit dx scalar spacing, 2-D returning a list of two arrays, axis selection, edge_order=2 second-order accurate boundaries, and integer input promoted to float.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:06:15.316720857Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T18:34:02.100234295Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_numpy-txzl","title":"Support N-D fftshift and ifftshift parity","description":"Fallback parity work: np.fft.fftshift/ifftshift support N-D arrays and optional axes, but UFuncArray currently rejects non-1-D inputs. Implement all-axis N-D shifting plus axis-specific helper methods while preserving integer sidecars.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-18T02:32:05.514945745Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T02:42:05.797950882Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T02:42:05.796121469Z","close_reason":"Implemented N-D fftshift/ifftshift parity in f21c72f with all-axis default behavior, selected-axis helpers, and sidecar preservation tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fft","parity","ufunc"]}
 {"id":"franken_numpy-u089","title":"numpy.tri python wrapper parity","description":"Add fnp_python.tri passthrough wrapper around numpy.tri(N, M=None, k=0, dtype=float). Returns an array with ones at and below the k-th diagonal and zeros elsewhere. Include oracle test covering: tri(3) default, tri(3, 4) rectangular, k=1 above diagonal, k=-1 below diagonal, dtype=int, and verifying lower-triangular structure.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T10:11:16.817062980Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T10:11:16.817062980Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_numpy-u1zf","title":"Investigate Beads integrity_check warnings on freshly rebuilt DBs","description":"`br doctor --json` reports `sqlite.integrity_check` warnings (`page ... is never used`) on the canonical `.beads/beads.db` and also on a freshly rebuilt temp DB imported from healthy JSONL. `br show`, `br sync --status`, and rollback-only write probes all succeed, so this may be a frankensqlite/br diagnostic issue rather than repo-local corruption. Reproduce in isolation and determine whether repair, vacuum/checkpoint, or upstream fix is required.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-31T03:03:44.813529433Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T03:06:45.143864391Z","closed_at":"2026-03-31T03:06:45.143561397Z","close_reason":"Rebuilt a fresh DB from JSONL, proved  +  clears the integrity_check warning, promoted the cleaned candidate non-destructively, then applied the same cleanup on the canonical path. .gitignore so .beads inner ignore rules work   [● P1 · CLOSED]
Owner: ubuntu · Type: bug
Created: 2026-03-31 · Updated: 2026-03-31
Closed: 2026-03-31 (Removed the blanket root .beads/ ignore, preserved specific local-only Beads artifact ignores, and verified br doctor
OK jsonl.merge_artifacts
OK gitignore.beads_inner
OK sync_jsonl_path: JSONL path is within sync allowlist
OK sync_conflict_markers: No merge conflict markers found
OK jsonl.parse: Parsed 195 records
OK db.recovery_artifacts
WARN db.sidecars: WAL sidecar exists without a matching SHM sidecar at /data/projects/franken_numpy/.beads/beads.db-wal (expected for frankensqlite)
OK schema.tables
OK schema.columns
OK db.recoverable_anomalies
OK db.null_defaults
WARN sqlite.integrity_check: database disk image is malformed: page 53 is never used
OK counts.db_vs_jsonl: Both have 195 records
OK sync.metadata: Database and JSONL are in sync
WARN db.write_probe: Failed to begin rollback-only write probe: database is busy
WARN sqlite3.integrity_check: sqlite3 not available; skipping orthogonal integrity validation now reports gitignore.beads_inner=ok.)

`br doctor` warns that root `.gitignore` excludes `.beads/`, which prevents `.beads/.gitignore` from governing Beads runtime artifacts. This conflicts with the repo workflow that stages `.beads/` state in git. Fix the root ignore rule so tracked Beads files are not globally suppressed while preserving ignores for local-only sidecars/history., and {

} are now healthy on the default DB.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
